# DojoGenesis Logwatcher

> Zero-dependency Go log watcher with bounded fan-out, seek-to-EOF, and context-driven lifecycle.

## What this is

Logwatcher is a focused, zero-dependency Go library for tailing log files and broadcasting lines to multiple concurrent subscribers. It implements bounded fan-out pub-sub: a single goroutine reads from a file tail, and multiple subscriber channels receive each line. Slow subscribers are silently dropped (lines discarded) rather than blocking the fan-out. Lifecycle is managed entirely through `context.Context` — cancel the context to stop all watchers and clean up goroutines. Designed for embedding in Go services that need to observe log output in real time.

## Key capabilities

- File tail with seek-to-EOF on start (no replay of existing content by default)
- Multi-subscriber pub-sub: N readers from a single file watcher goroutine
- Bounded fan-out: slow or blocked subscribers are silently dropped, not back-pressured
- Context-driven lifecycle: pass a `context.Context` to control start and shutdown
- Zero dependencies: pure Go stdlib, no third-party packages
- Safe for concurrent use across goroutines
- Designed for embedding in observability pipelines and log aggregation services

## How to use with LLMs / agents

Import as a Go library. Create a watcher with a file path and context, subscribe channels to receive log lines, and use the output in your agent observability pipeline. Pairs well with the DojoGenesis Gateway SSE `/events` stream for bridging file-based logs into agent-visible event streams.

## Usage pattern

```go
watcher := logwatcher.New("/var/log/app.log", ctx)
ch := watcher.Subscribe()
go watcher.Watch()
for line := range ch {
    // handle log line
}
```

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/DojoGenesis/logwatcher
- Gateway (consumer): https://github.com/DojoGenesis/gateway
